cohort_notes: 30 subjects have verbalwm EEG plus embedded PPG and ECG recordings
22 subjects also have rest EEG plus embedded PPG and ECG recordings
8 subjects are verbalwm-only and match participants.tsv RS_excluded=yes

blocked_on: run a shift-aware or scale-robust baseline on the cohort-swap split before promoting more verbalwm subjects
decide whether the DS006848 rest branch should stay smoke-only or expand into a real rest benchmark
decide the long-term roles of DS006848 and DS003838 now that both broader verbalwm splits fail to beat null

notes: Candidate public EEG-PPG dataset for rest and verbal working-memory experiments.
Based on source-backed verification of the OpenNeuro 1.0.0 snapshot on April 4, 2026.
The source tree contains 30 subject folders and no session subdirectories.
All 30 subjects expose an eeg subtree with BrainVision payloads plus CapTrak electrodes and coordsystem sidecars.
EEG is stored in BrainVision files with 63 EEG channels at 1000 Hz.
PPG is stored as the PPG channel inside the eeg BrainVision container, alongside a single ECG channel, both sampled at 1000 Hz.
Task labels use rest and verbalwm in the source tree, and verbalwm maps to the internal working-memory benchmark family.
Behavior files are present only for verbalwm and expose 200 trials per sampled subject across Simultaneous, Fast, Slow, and Fast+delay conditions.
A first DS006848 verbalwm Phase 2 pilot artifact exists for sub-001 train and sub-007 eval, with 512 paired windows, zero alignment residual, and full quality-pass.
An expanded DS006848 verbalwm development artifact exists for sub-001 and sub-010 train versus sub-007 and sub-012 eval, with 1024 paired quality-pass windows.

A broader DS006848 verbalwm development artifact exists for sub-001, sub-010, sub-013, and sub-015 train versus sub-007, sub-012, sub-016, and sub-017 eval, with 2048 paired windows and 1983 quality-pass windows.
The broader DS006848 target artifact keeps dominant-beat coverage dense with 1978 valid windows and introduces the first nonzero DS006848 eval notch coverage with 123 valid windows.
The broader DS006848 morphology baseline no longer beats the null on aggregate standardized MSE for either EEG branch, with eeg_clean now the least-bad branch.

A machine-readable subject-quality policy marks sub-016 as stress-test-only and sub-017 as borderline-review within the current reviewed verbalwm cohort, while the remaining verbalwm subjects stay pending review.
A first DS006848 rest Phase 2 pilot artifact now exists for sub-001 train and sub-007 eval, with 20 paired windows, zero alignment residual, and full quality-pass.
A first DS006848 rest target artifact now exists on top of that rest pilot, with dense dominant-beat coverage in both train and eval and train-only notch coverage.
A broader 12-subject DS006848 verbalwm waveform-quality review now exists, with 1536 sampled windows, 1509 quality-pass windows, and a pending-review shortlist led by sub-002 and sub-035.

A first model-aware cohort-swap artifact now exists for sub-001, sub-010, sub-013, and sub-015 train versus sub-002, sub-007, sub-012, and sub-035 eval, with 2048 paired windows and 2048 quality-pass windows.
That cohort swap sharply reduces the broader DS006848 failure but still does not recover a null-beating aggregate baseline, with eeg_clean remaining the least-bad branch.
